feed.title: Amit Sharma
feed.subtitle: Data Analyst | Data Engineer | Data Scientist

entry.title: Corona Contemplation
entry.content:
I think we all can agree that our ideas and/or beliefs are the outcome of the interactions we have with our surroundings. Information we come across has a great effect in determining what we think is correct. I would like to present a case, an optimistic one, on coronavirus that got enormous attention because of bias we developed through the information presented to us. It is entirely possible that I, still do not have the entire picture and I might have missed the bigger picture but I would like to present a summary of how I thought the situation evolved.

We all have some ideas about the evolution of the crisis. It started from the bat in China, China locked itself down then followed the world. There were significant deaths in China, followed by Italy, Spain, the USA, the UK which led governments to panic and made them lock the country down. People were, and to this date are, asked to keep a distance from each other, avoid physical contact and go out of their house only if necessary.

A couple of reasons that caused panic did not sit right with me. Some of them were:

  1. Asymptomatic Carriers.
  2. Unavailability of testing kits.
  3. Panic on case discovery.
  4. Death and perception of Death.
  5. Reporting Mechanism.

This virus is new and methods for properly testing the COVID-19 virus are not known. This caused paucity of testing kits, they were not available when they were most required. Now, how can infection be found if there are no ways to determine there has been an occurrence. Quarantine was applied long after China started quarantine. Transportation was free all around the world until February. It was not until the first week of March when feeling for lockdown and travel restrictions started to feel like the right decision. Considering the global mobility and how effectively the virus could spread, a lot of people might have been already affected by it.

When people started getting themselves tested, some got positive results, some got negative. It was a relief for those who got negative but those with positive results seemed like odd cases (obviously, because the virus is new). Especially because the virus was asymptomatic and could remain so for up to 2 weeks. This means a person could infect as many people they come in contact with for 2 weeks and feel something that would make them go get tested. And we have to be aware that we were not conscious of the concept of physical distancing at the moment. This simple mechanism, asymptomatic carrier, points to the fact that there should be many people who have been affected by it. This can be vindicated by the post-lockdown infection numbers that kept coming.

This was followed by the statistics of death. I remember people being placed in ventilators because the virus seemed to affect the lungs and its capacity. The practice of placing people on a ventilator was revisited after it came to light that this process was harming the patients. This created bias as to what the virus can do. The virus seems to badly affect the people with already severe health conditions, it makes the most sense to assume that people with severe health conditions go for testing. If the people with already severe health conditions undergo complications, they end up living final moments of their life on the hospital bed. But this would be a small fraction of a population that was infected. Because the symptoms of the virus are similar to that of flu, it is possible that many did not test assuming it was flu and those who got better should already have immunity against the virus. This leads to a simple conclusion, there should already be herd immunity in many places, we just don’t know it yet.

We know that a huge number of people breathe their last breath because of the flu. We know the cases of a person suffering from AIDS can develop severe flu and end up dying from it. In this case, we cannot say that the flu was the reason for that person’s death, it was AIDS. With the enormous attention that coronavirus has got, I have not read the reports, coronavirus may have been wrongly accused of costing the lives of people when the real culprit might be some other health condition or conditions.

In addition to that, when a person is tested for coronavirus, reports take a certain number of days (4-9 days) to be available. So, when we see a surge in virus cases on Thursday, what it means is a bunch of people tested some days ago and some of their reports came today. It could mean that testing done 4-9 days ago could have come today.

This could all be regarded as speculation but data seem to point in the same direction. It is only a matter of time when we will see the actual severity and effect of virus.
entry.summary: I think we all can agree that our ideas and/or beliefs are the outcome of the interactions we have with our surroundings. Information we come across has a great effect in determining what we think is correct. I would like to present a case, an optimistic one, on coronavirus that got enormous attention because of bias we developed through the information presented to us. It is entirely possible that I, still do not have the entire picture and I might have missed the bigger picture but I would like to present a summary of how I thought the situation evolved.

entry.title: 600 pushups everyday!
entry.content:
When I started doing pushups seriously, which is at around 2011, I could hardly do 37 straight. I remember, on my thirty-eighth attempt my arms were shaking and I could feel the sweat flowing from my forehead to the tip of my nose. I would try to push myself upwards with my shivering arms while the sweat accumulated on the tip of my nose and dropped. This was followed by me dropping flat on my chest.

As the days went by, I persisted and tried my best. These days, I can easily do at least 50 pushups without sweating a lot, on my bad day. It took a journey to get where I am now. I was constantly dedicated and passionate to get the number higher.

There are a couple of ideas to follow if you want to succeed in being able to increase the number of pushups. I want to share my opinions on the process of progressing from 38 pushups to 600 a day. I was not able to achieve physical prowess in a short period; it took me around 3 months to properly grow after I realized that my initial process was ineffective.

Following are my opinions:

  • Pick A Time .i.e. Discipline: You must set aside a time of the day that works the best for you. When I say, “The Best For You”, I mean you should make sure that specific time slot is almost always free. I recommend you do your training after your office hours because usually one is happy with what one did at work and can train better. Contrary to that, sometimes you have frustrations due to work and while you are frustrated, you tend to train longer and harder. That is what happens to me at least. Most importantly, training will reduce the frustrations you developed during the work and if you are happy it makes you happier. I will seem like you are wasting precious time doing an unproductive activity working out, but this activity will increase your productivity.
  • Commit: After that you have set aside a time, you must commit to it. No excuses. As I mentioned earlier, there will be frustrations and you want to give up. In the beginning you may feel ecstatic and do not want to continue your training. In that case you can take a day off your normal schedule but try your best to not take more take 2 days off in a row.

As I am growing older I started to realize that you can use those ideas in other areas too, like education, work and maybe even relationship (this might be a little stretch but, it is possible).

You are now ready. If you pick a time of day to train and commit to it, you will see results.

Now, as for pushups, here is what I did.

My initial goal was to be able to do as many pushups in one go as possible (initial goal was 60). As I mentioned I could not do more than 37 on my first try. I persisted, I increased by 1 every day. My best increased to 47 in one week. After that, my best started to plateau. It did not increase as wildly as the first week for weeks. in the next 2 weeks, I reached 52. I remember my arms were still shaking just as hard as they were when I did the first time however I was sweating less (not sweating a lot felt good).

Reckoning that fact that I was not progressing much, I thought I might have been doing something wrong. I had noticed that my pectoral muscles were developing faster because of the training. Towards the end of every training, I would strain myself a little too hard and I could feel my abs and my lats were tightening.

Change the Plan, Never the Goal

I did not see much progress, so I changed my work out tactics. Instead of focusing on doing a lot of pushups, I tried to do it in sets. What do I mean by this? I was able to barely do around 50 pushups straight, right? Now, I changed the way I did it and started doing 4 sets of 35 pushups. I fixed the interval between sets, so that I do not exceed 120 seconds (believe me, that is plenty of time when you get used to it).

NEVER GIVE UP

In the beginning it was challenging but now I was doing 140 pushups instead of 52. It took a lot of time to complete them but once I started, I did not stop. During a set, when I felt like next repetition would be impossible to perform, I would just hold until I felt confident to do the next one. I was determined to not fall flat on my chest and I was determined to complete 35.

FRUITS OF NOT GIVING UP

As the days went by, I could easily do 4 sets of 35. It was not challenging enough. Now I increased the number of pushups per set to 40, and soon 40 was not challenging enough. Soon, I was mixing the numbers os repetitions by starting my first set with 35 pushups, continue to the second set with 45 pushups, the third set with 55 pushups and end it with the last set of 70 pushups. The time interval between the first and second set was 60 seconds, 90 seconds between the second and third set, and 120 seconds between the last 2 sets. Most of the time I would not warm up and just start a full blast, I would go all 4 sets with 60 pushups with at most 90 seconds break in between.

It is an intensive short-duration training if you follow it.

SO HOW DID I DO 600 PUSHUPS??

Soon after my pushups stamina increased dramatically, I did following:

  • Start a timer.
  • Immediately start pushups. Do 50 of them.
  • Stop after 50 pushup.
  • Rest until timer hits 5 minute.
  • Start pushups when timer hits 5 minute. Complete 50 pushups.
  • Rest until timer hits 10 minute.
  • Start pushups when timer hits 10 minute. Complete 50 pushups.
  • Continue this for an hour.

Notice that I do not stop the timer. It meant the longer I take to complete 50 pushups, the less rest I get between sets.

The more I did, the simpler it got to complete 600 pushups. Eventually, I went on to mix squats, barbell rows, biceps curl, and abs-roll-out in between the sets. You can try any of the ways to work out.

PERSISTENCE IS THE KEY!

entry.title: CNN (Convolutional Neural Network)
entry.content:
Introduction

A convolutional neural network is a special type of neural network. It is special in terms of its application .i.e. it is almost exclusively used in image classification. The word “Convolution” in Convolution Neural Network (from here on referred as CNN) refers to the operation done on the image we read or, intend to process for learning. Neural Network follows this convolution operation and hence we got the name, convolution neural network. To better understand CNN one should be comfortable with the idea that image is nothing but a matrix of numbers. Whenever we process an image, we are reading, processing, and studying those numbers.

To understand CNN one needs to understand some terms associated with it. Some common terms with a brief description is given below, detailed process on how those operations are carried out will be described later:

  • Convolution matrix or, kernel It is a matrix that extracts features from an image. The size and nature of the matrix depend on the application it is being used for.
  • Pooling It is done to carry out the downsampling of the feature matrix or, image obtained after convolution operation. Downsampling, in this case, refers to reducing the size of the image matrix. Idea behind pooling is very simple, from a given (n,n) matrix sample we select a value that is representative of that (n,n) portion. Convolution neural network is said to be indifferent to spatial information .i.e. cannot account for the position of the feature in an image.
  • Activation It determines what values pass and what does not pass to the neural network. It can be considered as a threshold for the result of a neuron to pass through it or, mapping of the output of the neural network. If the operation does not result in output that is above the threshold, it does not pass through to a neural network. For example, ReLU activation is max(0,x)

How to CNN

Image: Convolution neural network. In this case, the original image is passed through 5 convolution kernels/matrices of size n_1`x`n_1 and pooling. This results in an output that is n_1`x`n_1`x`5 to second convolution layer. This result is then passedthrough n_2`x`n_2`x`5`x`10 convolution kernels. Here n_2`x`n_2 is the size of convolution matrix, 5 is the depth of the convolution and 10 is the number of convolution kernels/matrices we have for this layer.

In the Image above, original image refers to the image under study, image we are going to feed to the neural network. For this instance let us consider how the network will work with a single image; a neural network will work with many such images to generate result (which will be extension of the single image’s case). "Convolution layer 1" refers to result of first convolution operation on Image. There are 5 of (n_1,n_1) sized kernels that work on "Original Image" to produce first convolution layer. "Conv layer 2" refers to second convolution layer. To get this, we have 10 kernels of size (n_2,n_2,5) that is applied to "convolution layer 1". Convolution operation is explained in upcoming sections.

A couple of ideas to get comfortable with while dealing with CNN is that an image is made up of numbers. It is the wildest combination of different numbers that are interpreted by a computer as color. A special feature of the convolution neural network is that it can extract specific features from the image and when we talk about features, it refers to a specific set of numbers that correspond to those features. So when we talk about CNN extracting features, we are identifying or, trying to identify the set of numbers that define the features we are interested in.

As mentioned above, CNN is a special type of neural network which has some special operation called convolution applied to input before moving forward to a neural network. Let us get into the convolution operation now.

Convolution

Let matrix “img” represent the image we want to analyze through neural network. We want to get efficient result and we have recently come across this tool called convolution and want to test its power. How we do it is following:

  • We have an image of certain size, say (n,n). Here (n,n) means that image's height and width are equal and are n pixels. Each color in the pixel is represented as a number and thus we get a (n,n) matrix.
  • There is a kernel matrix. Its size is strictly less than or, equal to the size of image matrix; .i.e. if kernel matrix is of size (m,m), then m < n. Kernels are responsible for identifying the features that neural network detects
  • During convolution operation, an extract of image matrix equal to the size of kernel matrix is taken, element-wise multiplication is carried out and sum of all elements of resulting matrix is taken. This resulting number is becomes an element of the Result matrix, one that is obtained after convolution operation.

The following steps show how we get each element of the Result matrix above. We first extract from the image matrix a matrix of size equal to that of kernel matrix, carryout element-wise multiplication, and add the result. That result is the first element of the Result matrix.

The following image from Stanford University gives a visual illustration of the convolution process.

Pooling

Kernel matrices combined with convolution operation identify or, accentuate certain features that we want the neural network to detect. The pooling operation detects those features. Convolution operation makes it easier to extract certain specific features from the image we are studying. This operation, pooling, down-sizes the image .i.e. reduces the dimension of the image, and at the same time takes the necessary information from the image. Just as the kernel matrix is mapped over the image matrix, a specific size for pooling is considered (say (2,2) or (3,3) or (4,4) matrix) and it is mapped over the result of the convolution operation.

Depending on the type of pooling operation used, we perform some computation. Some common pooling operation is max-pooling and average-pool. Max-pooling selects the maximum of all the elements in pool-size to result of convolution operation whereas average-pool takes an average of all the elements present in the pool size we are considering. Pool-size refers to the size of the matrix considered for pooling.

For the Result matrix above, is we consider a pool-sie of (2,2), we get first element that is maximum of Result_11, Result_12,Result_21,Result_22 .i.e. max(-4,-4,10,9) = 10`.

In case of average-pool, it would be average of Result_11, Result_12, Result_21, Result_22 .i.e.
(-4-4+10+9)/4 = 2.75.
